method_summary: The authors cluster pharmacokinetic curves using time series clustering methods, finding Euclidean distance to be the most appropriate dissimilarity measure for PK curves.
key_innovation: The first demonstration that unsupervised machine learning clustering of PK curves can independently validate pharmacogenomic conclusions without using subject genetic information.
datasets_used: 250 PK curves from a previous pharmacogenomic study

key_results.validation_capability: able to independently validate the same conclusions as the reference pharmacogenomic results

best_result: Unsupervised ML clustering with Euclidean distance was able to independently validate the same conclusions as the reference pharmacogenomic results without using genetic information
title: Clustering plasma concentration-time curves: applications of unsupervised learning in pharmacogenomics
